name: CSRF — Content-Type Bypass
educational_objective: Understand that some developers think checking Content-Type: application/json prevents CSRF. In reality, a form POST with application/x-www-form-urlencoded can carry the same payload, and the browser sends it cross-origin without preflight.
scenario: PayStream API — the /payment/execute endpoint was 'hardened' by requiring JSON body. However, the server parses both JSON and form-encoded bodies without strict Content-Type enforcement, making it bypassable via a simple form POST.
ar_scenario: PayStream API تفترض أن JSON فقط يعني أمان. لكن السيرفر يقبل form-encoded أيضاً — مما يسمح بتجاوز CSRF.

hints_metadata.content: The endpoint claims to require JSON. But does the server actually reject non-JSON bodies? And does the browser send a preflight for form-encoded requests?
hints_metadata.ar_content: النقطة تدّعي أنها تتطلب JSON. لكن هل السيرفر يرفض فعلاً الطلبات غير JSON؟

hints_metadata.content: application/x-www-form-urlencoded is a 'simple' content type — no CORS preflight. The browser sends it cross-origin directly. The server parses it even when expecting JSON.
hints_metadata.ar_content: application/x-www-form-urlencoded نوع 'بسيط' — لا يحتاج preflight. المتصفح يُرسله cross-origin مباشرةً.

hints_metadata.content: Click 'Simulate Victim' — this sends a form-encoded POST to /payment/execute. The server accepts it, processes the payment, and returns the flag.
hints_metadata.ar_content: اضغط 'Simulate Victim' — يُرسل POST بـ form-encoded. السيرفر يقبله ويعالج الدفع.

flag_validation.note: Returned when form-encoded body bypasses JSON-only assumption

hidden_solution_path.exploit_path: form POST application/x-www-form-urlencoded to /payment/execute → no preflight → server parses body → processes payment
hidden_solution_path.fix_summary: Strictly validate Content-Type server-side; check Origin/Referer headers; add CSRF token regardless of content type
